title: Who won the 2025-26 NHL Calder Memorial Trophy (Rookie of the Year)?
rules: If Ivan Demidov wins the NHL Calder Memorial Trophy in the 2025-26 NHL season, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] **Matthew Schaefer's Dominance:** The 18-year-old defenseman, selected first overall in the 2025 NHL Draft, has established himself as the overwhelming favorite throughout the season. Schaefer recorded 23 goals and 36 assists for 59 points across 82 games, rewriting NHL records for 18-year-old defensemen. He led all rookies in assists (36), power-play points (18), and average ice time (24:41), while becoming the first defenseman in over 90 years to lead rookies in goals. Betting markets reflected his dominance, with odds ranging from -650 to -1000 by late February. NHL.com's Trophy Tracker panel gave him 79 voting points (15 first-place votes) from a 16-person panel in late April. **Demidov's Strong Case:** The 20-year-old winger, fifth overall in the 2024 draft, led all rookies in points (62) and assists (43) across 82 games. He was named NHL Rookie of the Month for December 2025 after posting 14 points in 15 games. Demidov's elite processing and passing (1.78 assists per 60 at 5-on-5, sixth among all NHLers with 700+ minutes) made him a compelling alternative. He received 54 voting points (one first-place vote) in the Trophy Tracker panel, with betting odds around +850 by late February. **Sennecke's Offensive Production:** The 19-year-old Ducks forward tied Schaefer with 23 goals and added 37 assists for 60 points in 82 games. He became the first NHL rookie to reach 50 points this season and led rookies in even-strength points (46). Sennecke received 52 voting points in the Trophy Tracker panel, with +1000 betting odds. **Key Context:** Schaefer's candidacy was strengthened by the Islanders' playoff success and his record-setting production for a defenseman. However, Demidov's elite playmaking and Sennecke's balanced offensive contributions kept the race competitive through the regular season. **Most Important Facts:** (1) Matthew Schaefer dominated the Calder race with record-setting production for an 18-year-old defenseman and overwhelming betting market consensus (-1000 odds by late February). (2) Ivan Demidov led all rookies in total points and assists while playing reduced ice time, demonstrating elite efficiency. (3) All three finalists were announced May 5, 2026, with voting by the Professional Hockey Writers Association determining the winner.

args.reasoning: Strong, named, recent sources show this award is already resolved to Matthew Schaefer. On May 5, 2026, the NHL announced the three Calder Trophy finalists: Ivan Demidov (Canadiens), Matthew Schaefer (Islanders), and Beckett Sennecke (Ducks) (NHL.com finalists release). Subsequent award reporting confirms Schaefer won. Sportsnet reported on May 13, 2026 that Islanders defenseman Matthew Schaefer captured the Calder Trophy and noted he became the youngest winner at 18 years, 223 days. NHL.com’s awards coverage further states Schaefer was a unanimous selection, receiving all 198 first-place votes—the first unanimous Calder since Teemu Selanne in 1992‑93—eliminating ambiguity about the outcome. By contrast, Demidov and Sennecke were finalists but did not win; no other listed rookie was a finalist, and there are no countervailing reports of a different winner. Evidence shows the league and major outlets have declared Schaefer the winner; therefore the correct resolution is Matthew Schaefer. Upset check: a unanimous PHWA vote and official NHL announcement is definitive, leaving no room for reversal. Sanity: Calder winners are announced at the NHL Awards each spring; the named award coverage aligns with that cadence and cites specific voting details. Given these converging, official reports, Schaefer is the winner and all other outcomes should be near-zero. I assign the maximum allowed mass to Schaefer under constraints, with minimum residual to others.
